_provenance: Claude-authored original. DeepSeek not used. No official answer; rubric-scored estimate only.

tasks.promptZh: 假设你是李华。你的英国笔友 Tom 想了解你的校园生活。请根据以下要点给他写一封电子邮件：(1) 你最喜欢的科目及原因；(2) 一项你喜欢的课外活动；(3) 你在学校遇到的一个困难。词数 60-90，开头 Dear Tom，结尾署名 Li Hua。
tasks.prompt: You are Li Hua. Your British pen friend Tom wants to know about your school life. Write him an email (60-90 words). Audience: your pen friend Tom. Purpose: tell him about your daily life at school. Cover these key points: (1) your favourite subject and why; (2) one after-class activity you enjoy; (3) one difficulty you meet at school. Begin with 'Dear Tom' and sign as 'Li Hua'.
referencePoints: Email format correct: greeting (Dear Tom) and signature (Li Hua) both present
All three required content points clearly included
Simple present tense used correctly for routines and habits
Friendly, personal tone suitable for writing to a pen friend
Reference points guide scoring only and are not a model answer

tasks.promptZh: 假设你是学生会主席王林。学校将于下周五下午举办秋季运动会。请用英文写一则通知，告知全校学生。要点：(1) 时间和地点；(2) 主要活动；(3) 对学生的要求（穿运动服、准时到场）。词数 50-80。
tasks.prompt: You are Wang Lin, chair of the Student Union. The school will hold its autumn sports meeting next Friday afternoon. Write a notice in English to inform all students. Audience: the whole school. Purpose: announce the sports meeting. Include: (1) the time and place; (2) the main activities; (3) what students should do (wear sports clothes, arrive on time). Word limit 50-80. Use a notice format with a title and 'Student Union' at the end.
referencePoints: Notice format used: a title and the issuer (Student Union) shown
Time, place, activities and requirements all stated clearly
Imperative and future forms used appropriately for an announcement
Language is brief, clear and public in tone
Reference points are for scoring guidance, not a fixed answer

tasks.promptZh: 你今天放学后要去图书馆参加小组学习，晚饭可能晚回家。请给妈妈写一张英文留言条。要点：(1) 你去哪里、做什么；(2) 大约几点回家；(3) 让妈妈不要担心，并把饭菜留在桌上。词数 50-70。
tasks.prompt: After school today you are going to the library for group study and may be late for dinner. Write a short message in English to your mother. Audience: your mother. Purpose: tell her your plan so she will not worry. Include: (1) where you are going and what you will do; (2) about what time you will be home; (3) a kind request to leave your dinner on the table and not to worry. Word limit 50-70.
referencePoints: Message is addressed to mother and signed by the writer
All three points (place/activity, time home, reassurance) covered
Present continuous or 'be going to' used for planned actions
Warm, considerate family tone
Reference points support scoring and are not an official answer

tasks.promptZh: 学校英语角正在招募志愿者。请用英文写一段自我介绍来申请。要点：(1) 你的姓名、年级和性格特点；(2) 你的英语水平和兴趣爱好；(3) 你为什么想加入以及你能做什么。词数 60-90。
tasks.prompt: The school English Corner is looking for student volunteers. Write a short self-introduction in English to apply. Audience: the English Corner organizers. Purpose: persuade them to accept you as a volunteer. Include: (1) your name, grade and one personality strength; (2) your English level and hobbies; (3) why you want to join and what you can do to help. Word limit 60-90.
referencePoints: Self-introduction clearly states name, grade and a personal strength
English level, hobbies, motivation and contribution all addressed
Positive, persuasive tone suitable for an application
Adjectives of personality and present tense used correctly
Reference points guide scoring; no single correct wording is required

tasks.promptZh: 你的同班来了一名新同学 Mike，他刚到中国，对学校生活还不熟悉。请用英文给他写一封短信，给他三条适应学校生活的建议。要点可包括：交朋友、参加活动、遇到困难时找老师帮忙。词数 60-90，开头 Dear Mike，署名 Li Hua。
tasks.prompt: A new classmate, Mike, has just arrived in China and is not yet familiar with school life. Write him a short letter in English with three pieces of advice for fitting in. Audience: your new classmate Mike. Purpose: help him adjust to school. You may mention: making friends, joining activities, and asking teachers for help when in trouble. Word limit 60-90. Begin with 'Dear Mike' and sign as 'Li Hua'.
referencePoints: Letter format with greeting (Dear Mike) and signature (Li Hua)
Gives three clear, relevant pieces of advice
Uses suggestion structures such as 'You could', 'Why not', 'It is a good idea to'
Encouraging, welcoming tone
Reference points are scoring guidance, not a model letter
